Article Title: Ravichandran Ashwin endures tough MLC debut despite San Francisco Unicorns' victory
Article Text:
Ashwin endures tough MLC debut (Image: X)

Difficult start for Ashwin



Rossouw keeps the pressure On



Unicorns overcome Ashwin's expensive spell



Veteran India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin finally made his long-awaited Major League Cricket debut for the San Francisco Unicorns, but it proved to be a challenging outing for the 39-year-old as he went wicketless against the Texas Super Kings. Ashwin's appearance was a significant milestone, making him the first capped India men's international cricketer to feature in Major League Cricket. The off-spinner had missed the Unicorns' season opener as he continued his recovery from a knee injury that had previously forced him to withdraw from the Big Bash League.Although the San Francisco Unicorns eventually secured a comfortable seven-wicket victory, Ashwin struggled to make an impact with the ball. He finished with figures of 0/24 from two overs, conceding runs at an economy rate of 12.00.Introduced into the attack during the fourth over, Ashwin received a harsh welcome from Texas batter Milind Kumar. The veteran spinner's first delivery was slog-swept over fine leg for a six, immediately putting him under pressure. Milind continued the assault later in the over, launching another delivery straight down the ground for a second maximum.Ashwin's opening over yielded 15 runs, with Milind Kumar targeting the spinner's lengths and denying him any opportunity to settle into a rhythm.Ashwin returned for his second over in the 10th and found little respite. South African batter Rilee Rossouw rotated the strike effectively before dispatching a short delivery for a boundary down the ground. Rossouw and Wiaan Mulder ensured there were no wicket-taking opportunities for the off-spinner, collecting singles regularly and preventing Ashwin from building sustained pressure.By the end of his spell, Ashwin had bowled just two overs, conceding 24 runs without a breakthrough.Despite Ashwin's struggles, the San Francisco Unicorns' bowling attack delivered a disciplined collective performance. Veteran Australian pacer Peter Siddle led the charge with figures of 3/36, while captain Matthew Short chipped in with two wickets to restrict Texas Super Kings to 152/9 from their 20 overs.The Unicorns then completed the chase comfortably, thanks to an unbeaten 69 from Lhuan-dre Pretorius, who guided the side to a seven-wicket victory with 13 balls remaining.
Article Summary: Ravichandran Ashwin made his Major League Cricket debut for the San Francisco Unicorns. The 39-year-old went wicketless as the Unicorns beat the Texas Super Kings. Ashwin is the first capped India men's international cricketer to play in MLC.

Article Title: Lhuan-dre Pretorius stars as San Francisco Unicorns cruise past Texas Super Kings by seven wickets
Article Text:
Lhuan-dre Pretorius guides San Francisco Unicorns to comfortable win (Image credit: Sportzpics)

Siddle leads Unicorns' bowling charge



Pretorius anchors clinical chase



The San Francisco Unicorns registered their first victory of the 2026 Major League Cricket season with a commanding seven-wicket win over the Texas Super Kings at Grand Prairie Stadium in Dallas on Saturday.After winning the toss and electing to field, the Unicorns produced a disciplined bowling display to restrict Texas Super Kings to 152/9 in their allotted 20 overs. In reply, a composed unbeaten innings from opener Lhuan-dre Pretorius ensured San Francisco chased down the target with ease, reaching 153/3 in 17.5 overs.Texas Super Kings endured a difficult start after being put into bat. Their top order failed to capitalize on the powerplay as captain Faf du Plessis departed for just six runs, while Saiteja Mukkamalla managed only one before heading back to the pavilion.With the innings in danger of unraveling, Rilee Rossouw attempted to steady the ship. The South African left-hander struck 36 off 29 deliveries and looked set for a bigger score before a run-out cut short his stay at the crease.The Super Kings struggled to build momentum against a disciplined Unicorns bowling attack. Veteran Australian pacer Peter Siddle was the standout performer, claiming three wickets for 36 runs. Captain Matthew Short complemented him perfectly, returning figures of 2/25 from his four overs and ensuring Texas never gained complete control of the innings.A late flourish from Hardus Viljoen helped the Super Kings post a competitive total. The all-rounder remained unbeaten on 33 from 24 balls, guiding his side past the 150-run mark as they finished on 152/9.The Unicorns' chase began with an early setback when Finn Allen was dismissed for just one run by Nandre Burger in the opening over.However, Pretorius and Matthew Short quickly wrestled back control of the contest. The pair added 82 runs for the second wicket in just 47 deliveries, putting the pressure firmly back on the Super Kings bowlers. Short played an aggressive hand, smashing 31 off 19 balls before falling after providing the perfect platform. Connor Esterhuizen chipped in with a useful 20, while Sanjay Krishnamurthi remained unbeaten on 19 as he joined Pretorius in completing the chase.Pretorius finished unbeaten on 69 off 55 balls and was deservedly named Player of the Match for his match-winning knock.
Article Summary: San Francisco Unicorns beat Texas Super Kings by seven wickets. Lhuan-dre Pretorius scores unbeaten 69 to guide Unicorns to comfortable win. Peter Siddle takes three wickets for 36 runs as Super Kings struggle to build momentum. San Francisco reach 153/3 in 17.5 overs in chase.

Article Title: Not Rohit Sharma or Ellyse Perry: Harmanpreet Kaur set to become first cricketer in history to ...
Article Text:
Harmanpreet Kaur

Poll Which player do you think is the biggest influence in women cricket today? Harmanpreet Kaur Ellyse Perry Suzie Bates

India captain Harmanpreet Kaur is set to create a milestone on Sunday when she takes the field against South Africa in Manchester, becoming the first player — male or female — to appear in 200 T20 Internationals.The landmark comes during the Women's T20 World Cup and marks a journey that has stretched across 17 years, covering multiple generations of cricketers and the growth of women's cricket on the global stage.Ireland's Paul Stirling holds the record for playing most games in Men's T20Is (163), while Rohit Sharma is at number two in men's category with 159 T20Is.In women's T20Is, Kaur is currently at top with 199 games and she is followed by New Zealand's Suzie Bates (184). Australian legend Ellyse Perry is at nuber four in WT20Is with 177 games.Kaur made her T20I debut during the inaugural Women's T20 World Cup in England in 2009. Seventeen years later, she is preparing to play her 200th match in the format during the tournament's 10th edition. Only six other players who were active alongside her in 2009 are still playing international cricket.The 37-year-old will lead India in a key Group A clash against South Africa at Old Trafford.India bowling coach Aavishkar Salvi praised Kaur's contribution to the game and the team."I think Harman is a top-level athlete," he said. "She's a role model for almost all cricketers globally. The way she has conducted herself over the years she's been a role model throughout. She's been a performer in any format. She's playing her 200th T20, that's a big thing. And we are all as excited to watch her perform."Salvi also highlighted the role Kaur plays in guiding younger players."And work ethics-wise, she keeps on working on the basics. She shares a lot of experience with the youngsters. So that also is a dimension where she comes in. At times, there is a lot of information available, but coming from someone who has performed so well at the highest level, those little minor points which she shares with the players, that actually help boost the careers of a lot of youngsters," he added.South Africa all-rounder Chloe Tryon, who plays alongside Kaur for Mumbai Indians in the Women's Premier League, also acknowledged the Indian captain's achievement."200 games is a lot. She makes it sound easy. I've had the opportunity to play alongside her, watch her do her thing. It's been remarkable to watch her journey, when I was a small girl as well, just looking up to big players. She's been a role model for so many people around the world. To see her still playing cricket is amazing, and leading from the front, which is really good to see. Hopefully, she has 200 more. I don't know how many more she can push through, but look, it's great to see how the women's game has grown and how she's taken the game forward. "South Africa head into the contest needing a win after losing to Australia in their opening match in Manchester before beating Pakistan at Edgbaston.India, meanwhile, have won both their matches so far, defeating Pakistan and the Netherlands.Despite India's strong start to the tournament, South Africa have enjoyed the upper hand in recent meetings between the two sides. The Proteas recently won a five-match T20I series at home 4-1.
Article Summary: India captain Harmanpreet Kaur set to become first player to play 200 T20 Internationals. The 37-year-old made her T20I debut during the inaugural Women's T20 World Cup in England in 2009. Kaur is currently at top with 199 games and she is followed by New Zealand's Suzie Bates.

Article Title: Japan make World Cup history with emphatic 4-0 win over Tunisia
Article Text:
Japan past Tunisia (AP Photo/Sofia Yaker)

Japan produced one of the standout performances of the FIFA World Cup 2026 so far, dismantling Tunisia 4-0 in a one-sided Group F encounter at Estadio Monterrey on Sunday. The victory not only strengthened the Samurai Blue's grip on qualification but also marked a historic occasion as the match became the 1,000th fixture in FIFA World Cup history.From the opening whistle, Japan looked a class apart. The Asian giants took just four minutes to break the deadlock when Daichi Kamada finished off a flowing team move. Keito Nakamura burst into the penalty area and drilled a low ball across goal, allowing Kamada to bundle home from close range. The strike became Japan's fastest-ever goal in World Cup history and set the tone for the rest of the evening.Tunisia struggled to cope with Japan's pace and movement throughout the first half. The North Africans spent long spells chasing the ball as Japan controlled possession and dictated the tempo. Goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen briefly kept Tunisia in the contest with an excellent save to deny Ayase Ueda, but the pressure continued to mount.Japan eventually doubled their advantage in the 31st minute through Ueda. The striker collected the ball on the edge of the area before firing a precise low effort beyond Dahmen and into the bottom corner.Tunisia offered little in response and headed into the break fortunate to trail by only two goals.The pattern of the match remained unchanged after halftime. Japan continued to dominate possession while Tunisia struggled to create clear-cut opportunities. Hannibal Mejbri attempted to spark a response for the Eagles of Carthage, but Japan's organised defence comfortably dealt with the threat.Any lingering hopes of a comeback disappeared in the 69th minute. Ueda turned provider this time, releasing Junya Ito with a clever pass. The winger shrugged off Mohamed Amine Ben Hamida before calmly slotting the ball underneath the advancing Dahmen to make it 3-0.Japan completed the rout seven minutes from time when Ueda capped off a memorable evening with his second goal. Rising highest inside the box, the striker guided a looping header into the net despite several Tunisian defenders attempting to clear off the line. The brace also saw Ueda become the first Japanese player to score twice in a single FIFA World Cup match.The result delivered another landmark moment for Japanese football, as they registered their first-ever four-goal performance at a World Cup. More importantly, the victory leaves Hajime Moriyasu's side needing only a point from their final group-stage match against Sweden to secure automatic qualification for the Round of 32.For Tunisia, the heavy defeat leaves their tournament hopes hanging by a thread.
Article Summary: Japan defeated Tunisia 4-0 in a one-sided Group F encounter at Estadio Monterrey on Sunday. The victory strengthened the Samurai Blue's grip on qualification but also marked a historic occasion as the match became the 1,000th fixture in FIFA World Cup history.

Article Title: Explained: Why Japan striker Ayase Ueda 'barely celebrated' despite scoring his first 2 World Cup goals in one game
Article Text:
Japan's Ayase Ueda (18) celebrates after scoring their second goal during the World Cup Group F soccer match between Tunisia and Japan in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Sofia Yaker)

Eredivisie's leading scorer struck twice against Tunisia at the World Cup, yet Japan's forward says the moments after scoring are never really about him.

Japan's Ayase Ueda, left, and Daichi Kamada celebrate after Ueda scored their second goal during the World Cup Group F soccer match between Tunisia and Japan in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Sofia Yaker)

A performance that helped shape a historic night for Japan

Tunisia goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen fails to stop Japan's Ayase Ueda from scoring his team's second goal during a World Cup Group F soccer match in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Moises Castillo)

Japan's Ayase Ueda, center, scores their fourth goal with a header against Tunisia during the World Cup Group F soccer match in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)

Family remains at the centre of Ueda's football journey

Japan's Ayase Ueda celebrates scoring his side's second goal against Tunisia during the World Cup Group F soccer match in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)

Japan strengthen their position in Group F

Japan players acknowledge supporters after the World Cup Group F soccer match between Tunisia and Japan in Guadalupe, near Monterrey, Mexico, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo/Addison Simmons)

Japan's 4-0 victory over Tunisia at the 2026 FIFA World Cup delivered another statement performance from a side that continues to emerge as one of the tournament's most impressive teams. Daichi Kamada opened the scoring, Junya Ito added another after the interval, and Ayase Ueda produced a clinical brace as the Samurai Blue moved level on points with the Netherlands in Group F.Yet while Ueda's goals helped drive Japan towards the knockout stages, the 27-year-old forward remains one of the more understated figures among the tournament's leading scorers.Ueda arrived at the World Cup as the leading scorer in the Eredivisie and enhanced that reputation again against Tunisia, scoring his first two World Cup goals in a single match. You would expect a wild celebration on football’s biggest stage, but his reaction after finding the net stood in contrast to the increasingly elaborate celebrations that often accompany goals at this level.There were no choreographed routines, no theatrical gestures towards the crowd and no attempts to turn the spotlight entirely onto himself. Instead, Ueda responded in the same manner that has become familiar throughout his career, offering a calm gesture of appreciation before quickly returning his focus to the match.Speaking previously in an interview with Dutch newspaper Algemeen Dagblad, Ueda explained why those moments matter differently to him."It's my style, my modesty," Ueda said. "When I've scored, I hear the fans and their joy. I let that sink in. I also think of my wife who has always supported me."While the celebrations remained restrained, Ueda's contribution against Tunisia was decisive, not only because of the quality of his finishing but because the goals represented a personal milestone on football's biggest stage.The two strikes against Tunisia were the first FIFA World Cup goals of Ueda's career. Although he featured at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar and made his tournament debut in Japan's group-stage match against Costa Rica, where he played 45 minutes, he left that competition without scoring. His breakthrough finally arrived four years later in what was also the 1,000th match in World Cup history.Japan established control of the match early at Monterrey Stadium when Keito Nakamura supplied a precise pass for Daichi Kamada, who calmly converted in the fourth minute to hand Hajime Moriyasu's side an immediate advantage.Japan's second goal arrived in the 31st minute and showcased the qualities that have made Ueda one of Europe's most productive forwards. Defender Ko Itakura delivered a pinpoint long-range ball into the penalty area, allowing Ueda to time his run perfectly, beat the Tunisian defensive line, bring the ball under control and finish clinically beyond the goalkeeper.The match continued to tilt heavily in Japan's favour as the second half unfolded. In the 69th minute Ueda turned provider, supplying Junya Ito for Japan's third goal of the evening before returning to the scoresheet himself in the closing stages.Ayase Ueda’s second goal of the match in the 83rd minute saw Japan midfielder Kaishū Sano drive forward from midfield as the Samurai Blue built a flowing move down the right flank, with sharp pass-and-move play opening up Tunisia’s defence. Sano then delivered a perfectly weighted cross toward the far post, where the Feyenoord striker climbed high above his markers, timing his leap exquisitely, and guided a looping header over goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen and the scrambling defenders into the net to complete his brace and Japan’s 4-0 victory.The result carried significance beyond the three points. Japan became the first Asian nation in FIFA World Cup history to score four goals in a single World Cup match, adding another landmark achievement to an increasingly impressive campaign.The performance also continued a rich vein of form that had already established Ueda as one of Japan's most important attacking players before the tournament began. The Feyenoord forward entered the 2026 FIFA World Cup with 38 senior caps and 16 international goals for Japan. Following appearances against the Netherlands and Tunisia, including his brace against the latter, Ueda's international record now stands at 40 caps and 18 goals for the Samurai Blue.His influence had been particularly evident during Japan's qualification campaign. Ueda finished as the Samurai Blue's leading scorer during the AFC qualifying rounds, scoring eight goals in nine appearances. Among the highlights were a hat-trick against Myanmar in the opening phase of qualification and a clinical brace against Syria as Japan secured their place at the finals.He also carried momentum into the tournament from the international friendlies that preceded it. Ueda scored twice in Japan's World Cup preparations, including the match-winning header in a dramatic 3-2 comeback victory over Brazil in Tokyo, a result that further reinforced his growing importance to Moriyasu's side.According to USA Today, Ueda's perspective on scoring is closely connected to his upbringing in Mito, Ibaraki, and the sacrifices made by those around him throughout his development as a footballer.Rather than viewing goals as personal achievements, he sees them as moments that belong to the people who helped him reach the highest level of the game.His father played a major role in introducing him to football, while his wider family provided support throughout the years required to progress from a promising youngster into an international striker competing on the World Cup stage.That background continues to shape the way he approaches success."My family in Japan too," Ueda said. "That I am here now thanks to them. I am very happy when I score, but I especially like that I can make others happy."For supporters watching from the stands or on television, the celebration may appear understated compared with many of football's more recognisable goal celebrations.For Ueda, however, the gesture serves a specific purpose.The moment is not reserved for personal recognition. It is an acknowledgement of family members back home, teammates on the pitch, supporters in the stadium and everyone who contributed to the journey that brought him to the World Cup.Even after scoring his first World Cup goals and helping Japan produce a historic result, his reaction remained unchanged. The expression was calm, the gesture restrained, but the meaning behind it reflected years of gratitude towards those who helped him reach this stage.The victory over Tunisia significantly strengthened Japan's position in one of the tournament's most competitive groups.The Netherlands currently sit top of Group F with four points and a plus-four goal difference, holding the tiebreaker advantage because they have scored seven goals compared with Japan's six. The two nations remain level on points after their entertaining 2-2 draw earlier in the tournament.Japan occupy second place, also with four points and an identical plus-four goal difference. Sweden remain in contention with three points, while Tunisia's second defeat leaves them bottom of the group with zero points and officially eliminated from the competition.As the race for qualification enters its final stage, Japan's chances of reaching the knockout rounds have been strengthened considerably.For Ueda, meanwhile, another productive night at the World Cup brought more evidence of his importance to the national team. The goals against Tunisia were not only his first on the World Cup stage but the latest chapter in a scoring run that stretches from qualification through the tournament itself.
Article Summary: Eredivisie's leading scorer struck twice against Tunisia at the World Cup. Japan's forward says the moments after scoring are never really about him. Daichi Kamada opened the scoring, Junya Ito added another after the interval, and Ayase Ueda produced a clinical brace.

Article Title: Selecao settle nerves against Haiti but bigger tests loom; Ancelotti may have to reshuffle after Raphinha blow
Article Text:
Brazil confirm Raphinha injury (Image: X)

Bittersweet. That’s how a Brazil fan might have felt in the early hours of Saturday. It was still Friday night in Philadelphia when the Samba Boys eased some of the growing pressure on them with their first win of the World Cup — a reasonably comfortable 3-0 stroll against unfancied Haiti. They will still need to beat Scotland in midweek and hope Morocco do not build a better goal difference if they are to be sure of finishing top of Group C.But this isn’t the Brazil of yore — one spoilt for choice in almost every position, especially in the attacking half. A team working with relatively thin resources would have had reason to worry when Raphinha limped off midway through the first half with a hamstring problem.Even if it is not a tear — the Barcelona star’s condition is still being evaluated — it seems unlikely that the five-time champions will be able to avail of his services before the quarterfinals, provided they go that far.Raphinha’s partnership with Vinicius Junior, one of the chief protagonists of Saturday’s victory, was slowly taking shape when the blow came. Raphinha’s ability to spot space in tight corners and release Vini for his marauding runs would surely have been one of Carlo Ancelotti’s major weapons going into the World Cup.With that plan stalled for now, the Italian may have to reshuffle his pieces.Bournemouth’s Rayan came on for Raphinha and did a decent job, but Ancelotti will hope that the recuperating Neymar is fit before Brazil face Scotland. According to the Italian, the 34-year-old will do some individual training over the weekend and rejoin the squad on Monday.While that will bring some relief to Selecao supporters, the true bright spots were the form of Vini and striker Matheus Cunha. Real Madrid winger Vinicius, who scored against Morocco in Brazil’s opener, found the net again against Haiti and had a hand in all three goals. The 25-year-old has kept a low profile and has so far avoided unnecessary controversy. Clearly, there is an Ancelotti hand — extending from their Real Madrid days — keeping him in check.However, bigger tests await. Against more organised opposition, with stronger defenders prepared to test him physically and otherwise, it will be interesting to see how the temperamental Brazilian reacts. Cunha, meanwhile, brought a sense of calm to the proceedings with an opportunistic strike in the 23rd minute. For a No. 9, it is vital to be in the right place at the right time and the Manchester United player showed he can be a good fit. His second goal, a thumping left-footer after being released by Vini inside the box, gave Brazil the insurance they badly needed. Vini added the third in first-half stoppage time to put the game completely beyond Haiti. Crucially, it also allowed Brazil to play in a lower gear thereafter, with the aim of keeping themselves fresh for the battles ahead.Brazil’s midfield of Bruno Guimaraes, Casemiro and Lucas Paqueta had a fairly comfortable night. The defence, too, was not tested much. Gabriel’s goalline clearance with a bicycle kick in the second half suggested that the Arsenal man may have overcome the disappointment of missing the final penalty in the Champions League final against PSG. After last week’s opening draw against Morocco, Brazil’s journey in this World Cup has begun somewhat like that of a title aspirant at a tennis Grand Slam. They have the burden of winning seven games in a row, and World No. 83 Haiti were just the kind of early-round opponent they would have hoped for.
Article Summary: Raphinha limped off midway through the first half with a hamstring problem. The Barcelona star’s condition is still being evaluated. It seems unlikely that the five-time champions will be able to avail of his services before the quarterfinals, provided they go that far. Vini and Matheus Cunha were the true bright spots for Brazil.

Article Title: Asian teams make their mark at World Cup despite second-round reality check
Article Text:
Asian Football teams make strong start to FIFA World Cup 2026 (Image: ANI)

Asian countries are not just making up the numbers at the World Cup. For the first time, Asia has nine teams at an expanded World Cup. Pre-tournament murmurs suggested the increased numbers could dilute the competitive factor, but at least the first-round results suggest the fears were unfounded.Six of the nine teams picked up encouraging results: South Korea came from behind to edge past Czechia, Australia put it across Turkiye, Japan held highly rated Netherlands to an entertaining 2-2 with a last-gasp goal, while Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Iran took points off higher ranked opponents.Even Jordan and Uzbekistan were brave in their approach on their respective World Cup debuts.“I am not surprised at all,” said former Croatia coach Igor Stimac, who has considerable experience of coaching on the continent with spells in Iran, Qatar and India as national team coach for five years. “They showed a lot of heart with their performances. Jordan and Uzbekistan were enjoying football, playing their hearts. There was no parking the bus, regardless of the gulf in rankings.”Of course, it’s just the first round but like Shaji Prabhakaran says there’s no harm in celebrating what has been a “great opening period” for Asian football.“The start against European opponents was terrific,” Prabhakaran, executive committee member at the Asian Football Confederation (AFC), told TOI in a video call from the US.“Asian football has been on the rise. Japan and Korea have plenty of players in Europe. They are dangerous and I expect at least one Asian team to go far. Japan have been very consistent. Their engaging draw against Netherlands would have given them confidence that they can give anyone a run for their reputation at the World Cup. ”Prabhakaran said Asian teams no longer fear higherranked elite opposition, as evident from South Korea’s coming from behind to edge past Czechia 2-1, Australia putting it across Turkiye and Saudi Arabia’s hard-fought draw against Uruguay which leaves Group H deadlocked after Cabo Verde’s stunning draw against Spain. Yet, if the first round was full of hope, second round games proved to be a dampener.
Article Summary: Six of the nine teams picked up encouraging results. South Korea came from behind to edge past Czechia, Australia put it across Turkiye. Japan held highly rated Netherlands to an entertaining 2-2 with a last-gasp goal. Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Iran took points off higher ranked opponents.

Article Title: Unbeaten since 2006: Netherlands set FIFA World Cup record with 14th straight game without loss, thrash Sweden 5-1
Article Text:
Netherlands' Brian Brobbey vies for the ball with Sweden's Victor Lindelof, left, during the World Cup Group F soccer match between the Netherlands and Sweden in Houston, Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP Photo)

Under pressure after an opening-game draw, the Netherlands responded with a 5-1 win over Sweden in the FIFA World Cup on Saturday to move to the top of Group F.Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each as the Dutch side secured its first win of the tournament. Brobbey struck twice in the first half to put the Netherlands in control before Gakpo added a brace to seal the result.The Netherlands had opened their campaign with a 2-2 draw against Japan and needed a win to strengthen their position in the group. The victory took them to four points, putting them ahead of Sweden, who remain on three points. Japan and Tunisia were scheduled to face each other later on Saturday.The result also saw the Netherlands set a record for the most consecutive World Cup matches without defeat, extending the run to 14 games. Although they were eliminated by Argentina in the quarterfinals of the 2022 World Cup, that match is officially recorded as a draw because the outcome was decided by a penalty shootout.The Dutch team's last outright World Cup defeat in regulation time came against Portugal in the round of 16 in Nuremberg in 2006.The Netherlands also extended another record, remaining unbeaten in 18 consecutive World Cup group-stage matches.Their last group-stage loss came against Belgium in 1994. However, the Dutch did not qualify for the 2002 and 2018 editions of the tournament.The five-goal display also helped the Netherlands reach another milestone. They became only the eighth team to score 100 goals in FIFA World Cup history, joining Argentina, Brazil, France, Italy, Germany, England and Spain.The Netherlands will conclude their Group F campaign against Tunisia in Kansas City on Thursday, while Sweden will take on Japan at Dallas Stadium on the same day.
Article Summary: Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 to move to the top of Group F. Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each as the Dutch side secured its first win of the tournament. The victory took them to four points, putting them ahead of Sweden, who remain on three points.

Article Title: USMNT icon delivers savage verdict on USA winning World Cup 2026: ‘Literally impossible’
Article Text:
US team poses during the World Cup Group D soccer match between the United States and Australia in Seattle, Friday, June 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Manu Fernandez)

USMNT's perfect start fuels growing belief



United States' Alex Freeman (16), second left, celebrates after scoring his side's second goal during the World Cup Group D soccer match between the United States and Australia in Seattle, Friday, June 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Manu Fernandez)

Donovan sees reasons for optimism



United States' Christian Pulisic (10) during the World Cup Group D soccer match between the United States and Australia in Seattle, Friday, June 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Manu Fernandez)

Zlatan Ibrahimovic takes a different view



Knockout rounds will provide the real test



The United States have become one of the stories of the opening week of FIFA World Cup 2026 after consecutive victories over Paraguay and Australia secured qualification for the Round of 32 with a game to spare. The co-hosts have scored six goals, conceded just once and become the first nation at the tournament to officially clinch top spot in their group.Yet despite the optimism surrounding Mauricio Pochettino's side, one of the most recognisable figures in United States football remains unconvinced that the team can mount a genuine challenge for the trophy.Former USMNT goalkeeper Tim Howard believes the idea of the United States winning the World Cup remains far beyond reach, even after their impressive start on home soil.Speaking on the "Unfiltered Soccer" podcast alongside former teammate Landon Donovan, Howard delivered a blunt assessment of America's chances."The US will need to play the greatest game they've ever played three times in a row," Howard said, before immediately correcting himself. "Sorry, four games in a row! ... It is literally impossible for the US to win the World Cup."His comments arrived at a moment when confidence around the national team has rarely been higher.The United States opened their tournament with an emphatic 4-1 victory over Paraguay before following it with a controlled 2-0 win against Australia in Seattle.The victory over Australia secured qualification for the knockout rounds and guaranteed first place in Group D after Paraguay defeated Türkiye later on the same matchday.The Americans have therefore achieved something they had not managed for nearly a century. Following victories against Paraguay and Australia, the USMNT recorded consecutive World Cup wins for the first time since the inaugural tournament in 1930.Those results have sparked increasing excitement among supporters, with some beginning to wonder whether a deep run could be possible on home soil.The team's rise has also been reflected in the rankings. The United States entered the tournament ranked 17th in FIFA's official pre-World Cup rankings released on June 11. Consecutive victories have lifted them to 13th in the live rankings, further reinforcing the growing sense of momentum surrounding Pochettino's squad.Howard, however, believes there remains a considerable difference between performing well in the group stage and winning football's biggest prize."That's just the reality," he said.Howard predicted group-stage success before Australia victoryHoward's scepticism regarding a potential World Cup triumph has not prevented him from being impressed by what he has seen so far.Before the Australia match, the former Everton and Manchester United goalkeeper predicted that the United States would finish top of the group."I still think we're going to win the game [against Australia]. I think we're going to win the group. I truly believe we're gonna win three games in this group," Howard said.At the same time, he also acknowledged that qualification scenarios could alter the approach for the final group-stage match against Türkiye."Now, there are scenarios… where, the US win this game [against Australia], they can clinch first place," Howard added."And if that happens, then I say [I'm] not sure [USA will win all three group-stage games], because I think we'll down tools and rest everybody against Türkiye, if we've already clinched first… In that case, then maybe [the USA doesn't] win three games."That exact scenario has now unfolded, but with a twist.The United States have already secured first place in Group D, leaving Pochettino with a decision over whether to rotate key players ahead of the knockout rounds.Türkiye, meanwhile, have already been eliminated after defeats against Australia and Paraguay.Howard's position is not universally shared, while Landon Donovan agreed that the current squad has not yet proven it can consistently defeat elite opposition, he believes the Americans are capable of competing with anyone left in the tournament.The former forward acknowledged that the biggest tests are still to come but stopped short of dismissing the team's chances entirely.Donovan said the United States "can absolutely compete against any team in this World Cup."His comments reflect the growing confidence generated by the team's performances during the opening two matches.Winning one knockout match would represent progress. Winning two would place the Americans deep into the latter stages of the competition and create one of the most memorable World Cup runs in the nation's history.Howard remains unconvinced that such momentum could ultimately lead all the way to the trophy.Howard's assessment contrasts sharply with the opinion offered by Zlatan Ibrahimovic during FOX Sports' World Cup coverage.Following the victory over Australia, presenter Rebecca Lowe asked the former Sweden striker whether the United States could win the tournament.Ibrahimovic's response consisted of a single word."Yes."The brief answer immediately generated discussion, particularly given the former striker's growing support for the American team during the tournament.For now, the United States have done everything asked of them. Pochettino's side comfortably defeated Paraguay, followed it with another professional performance against Australia and became the first team at the tournament to officially win its group.Christian Pulisic, Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun and the rest of the squad have given supporters genuine reasons for optimism heading into the knockout rounds.Whether that optimism eventually develops into a historic World Cup run remains the central question.
Article Summary: The United States have become one of the stories of the opening week of FIFA World Cup 2026 after consecutive victories over Paraguay and Australia. The co- hosts have scored six goals, conceded just once and become the first nation at the tournament to officially clinch top spot in their group. Former USMNT goalkeeper Tim Howard believes the idea of the United States winning the World Cup remains far beyond reach.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Manuel Neuer creates history as the most capped goalkeeper
Article Text:
In a remarkable feat, German goalkeeper Manuel Neuer has cemented his legacy in World Cup history by becoming the player with the most appearances, reaching his 21st match during Germany's gripping 2-1 win over Ivory Coast.(Photo credit: X)

Goalkeeper Country World Cup Matches Manuel Neuer Germany 21 Hugo Lloris France 20 Claudio Taffarel Brazil 18 Josef Maier Germany 18

German legendary shot-stopper Manuel Neuer became the goalkeeper with the most appearances in FIFA World Cup history on Sunday.When the 40-year-old Bayern Munich icon stepped out onto the pitch for Germany's high-octane Group E encounter against Ivory Coast in Toronto, it marked his 21st World Cup match. By doing so, Neuer surpassed the previous world record of 20 appearances, held by former French captain Hugo Lloris.Neuer had equalled Lloris’ milestone just days earlier during Germany’s clinical 7-1 opening victory against tournament debutants Curaçao. Taking his position between the posts against Ivory Coast officially pushed him into uncharted territory, standalone at the pinnacle of global goalkeeping greatness.The achievement adds another glittering chapter to an extraordinary international comeback. After stepping away from national team duty following Euro 2024, the 2014 World Cup winner reversed his retirement decision. Head coach Julian Nagelsmann immediately reinstated the veteran as Germany’s first-choice keeper for the expanded 48-team tournament in North America. At 40 years old, Neuer also stands as the oldest German player ever to feature at a major football tournament, breaking the record previously held by Lothar Matthäus.It was not a completely smooth historic night for the veteran, however. Ivory Coast refused to read the script early on, with skipper Franck Kessié hammering home an aggressive opening goal in the 30th minute to stun the Germans. Neuer had to stay alert and perfect for the remainder of the evening to deny the physical African side any further joy.Ultimately, substitute Deniz Undav saved the day, firing two late goals to secure a dramatic 2-1 victory. The win booked Germany's ticket into the Round of 32 with a match to spare, ensuring Neuer's record-breaking night was sweetened with all three points. Having played across five World Cup editions since his debut against Australia in 2010, the evergreen "sweeper-keeper" will now look to extend his historic tally even further.Goalkeepers with most World Cup appearances:
Article Summary: Manuel Neuer becomes the player with the most appearances in FIFA World Cup history. The 40-year-old reached his 21st match during Germany's 2-1 win over Ivory Coast. Neuer surpassed the previous world record of 20 appearances, held by former French captain Hugo Lloris.

Article Title: Super-Sub Deniz Undav equals Roger Milla’s historic World Cup record as Germany march into Round of 32
Article Text:
With an unforgettable performance, Deniz Undav struck twice to pull Germany back for a 2-1 win over Ivory Coast, securing their advance to the World Cup's Round of 32.(Photo/ AP)

German super-sub Deniz Undav turned into an absolute magician on Sunday, scripting a historic comeback victory for Germany against Ivory Coast while cementing his name right alongside the legendary Roger Milla in the World Cup record books.Coming off the bench in the 60th minute with Germany trailing 1-0 at the Toronto Stadium, the clinical forward completely turned the match on its head. Undav slammed home a magnificent volleyed equalizer in the 68th minute before showing nerves of steel to fire a dramatic 94th-minute stoppage-time winner, securing a hard-fought 2-1 win. The victory officially booked Germany’s ticket into the Round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a game to spare.While fans are marveling at his goal-scoring heroics, Undav's brace did something even more special as it vaulted him into elite football folklore. With his twin strikes against Ivory Coast, added to his explosive goal and two assists against Curacao in the opening game, Undav has racked up an unbelievable five goal involvements in just two substitute appearances.While it is frequently highlighted as a pure goal-scoring record, it is technically an equalization of the ultimate "super-sub" benchmark.Undav has officially matched Cameroon legend Roger Milla’s iconic 1990 record for the most total goal involvements as a substitute at a single World Cup since 1966. Milla captivated the world at age 38 in Italy with his clutch impact, and now Undav is replicating that exact magic for the four-time champions.Undav also became the first German international since Miroslav Klose in 2002 to find the back of the net in each of his first two career World Cup matches.Ivory Coast had initially stunned the Germans when captain Franck Kessie slotted home a rebound in the 30th minute. Germany also had two goals disallowed in a highly frustrating first half. However, coach Julian Nagelsmann’s decision to unleash his striker changed everything, keeping Germany's dream alive.
Article Summary: Deniz Undav struck twice to pull Germany back for a 2-1 win over Ivory Coast. The victory officially booked Germany’s ticket into the Round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a game to spare. With his twin strikes against Ivory Coast, Undav has racked up an unbelievable five goal involvements in just two substitute appearances.

Article Title: Did Blake Lively secure Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding invite? New report hints at friendship reunion
Article Text:
Blake Lively has reportedly secured an invitation to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's upcoming wedding after repairing her friendship with the singer. According to insiders, the relationship improved following the conclusion of Lively's legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Sources claim Swift has become more open to reconnecting, and Lively is once again welcome in her inner circle, although neither star has publicly confirmed the wedding invitation or reconciliation.

Taylor Swift and Blake Lively friendship reportedly moving forward

Taylor Swift and Blake Lively texts revealed emotional turning point

Months after speculation over the status of their friendship, Blake Lively is reportedly back in Taylor Swift 's inner circle and could be among the guests at the singer's highly anticipated wedding to NFL star Travis Kelce. Fresh reports suggest the pair have been rebuilding their relationship following the conclusion of Lively's legal dispute involving It Ends With Us director and co-star Justin Baldoni.The latest development has reignited interest in one of Hollywood's most closely watched celebrity friendships. While neither Swift nor Lively has publicly commented on the reports, multiple sources claim the relationship has improved significantly, opening the door for Lively to attend what is expected to be one of the biggest celebrity weddings in recent memory.According to DailyMail. com, people close to the situation believe the friendship is recovering after a difficult period. One source said Taylor was upset about becoming part of the legal drama but gradually became more open to reconnecting once the lawsuit drew to a close.A source told DailyMail. com: “Taylor was not happy about being dragged into the lawsuit but was more receptive to speaking to Blake as the suit came to a close,.Blake was ready to resume their friendship because it really was what caused its demise.“Everyone around Taylor is aware that Blake could attend. She may not, but that won't be because she isn't welcome.”Another source added: “Blake is on the cusp of getting a second chance, and they have had some loose conversations to test the waters to get the trust back. Blake is slowly getting back into the fold now that Taylor is having a change of heart.”A third insider also claimed Lively is conscious of how her attendance could be perceived, saying she does not “want to be seen as not going to the wedding because her image is very important to her”. The same source maintained that the pair “[They] never fully stopped being friends.”Although the reports remain unconfirmed, they suggest the relationship may be entering a more positive chapter after months of public speculation.The renewed reports come after previously unsealed court documents revealed a private text exchange between the longtime friends. The messages showed Lively expressing concern that she had unintentionally created distance in their relationship while dealing with personal and legal challenges.According to PEOPLE, Lively wrote:"I have no reason to ask, but I donno, I've been feeling like I should... is everything ok? I felt like a bad friend lately because I was such a sad sack who only talked about my own s*** for months. You were generous to not only be the key person there for me during all of it, but also to let me off the hook for being so in it."I always want the opportunity to be a better friend if there's something I unintentionally did. I know how busy and taxed you are - physically, emotionally, practically, so I don't expect any more from you ever. Just want to make sure all is good. (sic)"Swift later responded:"I think I'm just exhausted in every avenue of my life and in recent months had been feeling a little bit of a shift in the way you talk to me.Yes there has been a lot of Justin stuff but I've been through things like this before and I know how all consuming it is.""It's more like... and I feel really bad saying anything about this because your texts have been so nice in their intent but your last few... it's felt like I was reading a mass corporate email sent to 200 employees.""And it feels awful to be in any way critical of any way you process what you've been going through but I just kinda miss my funny, dark, normal-speaking friend who talks to me as herself... And I know you feel attacked from all sides for ridiculous reasons so you're feeling like you have to overly explain things... but. It's me! That's just caused a little distance. And you don't need to apologize. Just come back please. (sic)"The conversation offered fans a rare glimpse into how the two stars handled personal challenges behind closed doors. If the latest reports are accurate, that difficult chapter may now be firmly behind them as Swift prepares for her next major milestone with Travis Kelce
Article Summary: Sources claim the relationship improved following the conclusion of Lively's legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Neither star has publicly confirmed the wedding invitation or reconciliation. Lively is conscious of how her attendance could be perceived, saying she does not “want to be seen as not going to the wedding because her image is very important to her”

Article Text:
Rekindling Rumors: Dak Prescott and ex fiancee Sarah Jane Ramos board private jet to Miami reportedly to celebrate Father’s Day (Image via X: Sarah Jane Ramos and Dak Prescott)

Dak Prescott spends quality time with ex Sarah Jane Ramos and daughters for Father’s Day

Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos called off their wedding

The first reconciliation rumor came on Aurora Rayne Prescott’s first birthday

TMZ reports Dak Prescott , Sarah Jane Ramos, and their daughters headed to Miami on a private jet for a family trip on Friday, June 19.The former engaged couple could be spending the Father’s Day weekend together.Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos come together once again for Father’s Day.Father’s Day falls on the third Sunday of June every year.Prescott and Ramos were spotted leaving for Miami two days before Father’s Day.TMZ obtained photos of the duo boarding the plane with their precious daughters at McKinny National Airport around 11:45AM…And their final destination was Miami.Ramos is from Sunshine State, so it could be a family gathering.Sarah Jane Ramos and Dak Prescott were to wed on April 10, 2026, after a long courtship.But something happened during their joint bachelor/bachelorette event. They had a heated argument.Rumors spread saying Prescott was chatting inappropriately with many women using incognito social media profiles.Ramos had given him an ultimatum to stop all that and change his behavior. But to no avail!The next thing we know, they cordially decided to cancel the wedding.There were just a few days left before their wedding.Their representatives said they were both heartbroken with the decision.But then they sparked the first reconciliation rumor when they were both spotted a month later for their second daughter’s birthday.It was Aurora Rayne Prescott’s first birthday.Prescott and Ramos both decided to set aside their differences and come together to celebrate their daughter’s special day.Sarah shared several heartwarming snapshots on Instagram.“How is this perfect baby 1 today?” Ramos shared the photo on her Instagram Story with Prescott. “Grateful for the best day celebrating her.”The Dallas Cowboys quarterback reposted the photo on his Instagram Story with the caption, “Happy 1st to this Special Girl! You are loved.”Watching them come together, the fans quickly commented on Sarah’s IG post, saying, “They should really get back together -- they make such a beautiful couple.”Before the reconciliation rumor, Dak was spotted with Sarah’s former bridesmaid, Caitlin Rance, at the Pro Bull Riders event in Fort Worth, Texas.According to TMZ, eyewitnesses said they looked more than friends.Speculation about something brewing between the two began circulating in cyberspace.But TMZ got hold of the NFL star. Prescott told the outlet that nothing was going on between them, and they were just friends.So, does that mean that Sarah and Dak are back together?
Article Summary: Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos were spotted leaving for Miami two days before Father’s Day. The former engaged couple could be spending the Father's Day weekend together. Prescott and Ramos sparked the first reconciliation rumor when they were both spotted a month later for their second daughter's birthday.

Article Title: Buffalo Bills QB Josh Allen left speechless after reading wife Hailee Steinfeld’s heart humbling letter to baby girl Harper Haize Allen
Article Text:
Buffalo Bills QB Josh Allen left speechless after reading wife Hailee Steinfeld’s heart humbling letter to baby girl Harper Haize Allen (Image via X: Hailee Steinfeld)

Bills QB Josh Allen melts after reading wife Hailee Steinfeld’s letter for daughter Harper Haize Allen

Hailee Steinfeld’s lockscreen wallpaper is cozy couple vibe, picture perfect family, or favorite wedding photo

What started as --“Our baby girl has arrived!! We’re feeling incredibly grateful and blessed and savouring these early moments”has reached --“Daddy and I look at you often and ask each other the same question: How did we get so lucky to have you?”-- all in a span of almost 3 months! Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen revealed their beautiful baby daughter’s name as Harper Haize Allen via Beau Society.And when their declaration turned into an Instagram post,the Buffalo Bills quarterback reposted it, adding no words but just two emojis: “red heart” and “face holding back tears.”The Beau Society issue #90 arrived on June 20, 2026. It was titled, “A letter to my daughter…”Hailee Steinfeld revealed precious moments with daughter Harper Haize Allen.“You, whose tiny fingers already wrap around mine with a trust I pray I never take for granted.You, whose dad is officially outnumbered when it comes to choosing a movie for movie night, though I suspect he’ll secretly love every minute of it,” she wrote.The “Sinners” actress then shared how her daughter’s presence changed her with every passing day.“Each day with you, I meet a different version of myself.A softer version. A stronger version. A more patient and present version.A version that loves everything and everyone, including myself, more deeply than she ever knew possible,” she wrote.The NFL WAG realized how motherhood was not about big moments but tiny moments with a baby.“The sway of tired hips in the middle of the night, your little milk-drunk smiles,and the quiet realization, over and over again, that my heart is now somehow living outside of my body and it always will,” she wrote.In the #89 edition of the Beau Society, titled “YOU asked, I answered: Summer Edition!” the “True Grit” actress was asked by Meghan, “What’s your phone wallpaper right now?”Without further ado, the “Bumblebee” actress shared a screenshot of her phone lockscreen. It featured a cozy couple vibe shared between her and her husband, Josh Allen.The NFL MVP was wearing a short-sleeved monochrome floral graphic shirt.While the “Begin Again” actress was wearing a beige bodycon dress. Josh was wearing a hairband, while Hailee’s hair looked braided.And their body language? A warm hug that ended with Hailee’s hands around Josh’s shoulder and Josh dropping a steamy kiss on Hailee’s ear.Hailee answered, “My lock screen alternates between this, a picture of the three of us, and my favorite photo from our wedding.”
Article Summary: Bills QB Josh Allen left speechless after reading wife Hailee Steinfeld’s heart humbling letter to baby girl Harper Haize Allen. Buffalo Bills quarterback reposted it, adding no words but just two emojis: “red heart” and “face holding back tears”

Article Title: Jason Kelce’s wife Kylie Kelce stays out of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding spotlight by spending time in Paris Disneyland
Article Text:
(Image via Instagram: Kylie Kelce and Jason Kelce)

Kylie Kelce has no details about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding

Kylie Kelce spotted across pond from Swift-Kelce rumored wedding in Rhode Island

Jason Kelce’s wife, Kylie Kelce, is a bold, bold individual --when it comes to thrashing someone asking her for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce ’s wedding details.But when she’s surrounded by fans on a fun trip, she becomes the humblest person ever born on earth.She will take time out to pose with her fans. Where it’s due, she gives credit.But when she’s pestered, she will make sure she hits back like no other!Not long ago, when Travis Kelce’s mother, Donna Kelce, was swarmed by media for the Swift-Kelce wedding deets, Kylie Kelce spoke in favor of her mother-in-law.She firmly said, “Quit asking my mother-in-law and me about upcoming nuptials. Nobody’s f*ck*ng telling you anything. I don’t have details. I have no details. I have none.”She showed her flat, empty palm and said, “Look at that. That’s how many details I have. None. None!”The fierce tigress later went on to say, “Even if I did have any information, I’m not f*ck*ng telling you. That’s private information. Private information between family!”The 34-year-old mom-of-four even called out TMZ for swarming her mother-in-law at the LAX airport about Swift-Kelce’s upcoming nuptials.“If I had it? Private information between family! So, A and B see your way out. And while we’re talking about A and B and C and letters, TMZ? SMA!How about that? For anyone who’s confused about that – S*ck my a*s!”This was Kylie’s April 2026 chapter. Come June 2026, when the Travis-Taylor wedding is just miles away.She spends her quality time away from all the Swift-Kelce MSG [Madison Square Garden] attention at Disneyland Paris.But then Kylie’s timely presence there was also linked to Swift-Kelce’s alleged wedding in Rhode Island.E! News’ Lindsay Weinberg reports that amid Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s rumored wedding this weekend in Rhode Island, an important family member was spotted across the pond.Kylie Kelce was photographed on a stage at Disneyland Paris.She appeared to be filming an interview with rugby star Ilona Maher.A group trip host, Kasey Dellapenna, shared several snapshots of Kylie with Ilona from Disneyland Paris.She captioned the post: “I MET KYLIE KELCE AND ILONA MAHER AT DISNEYLAND PARIS TODAY!! BIG day for the Swiftie in me.”This was filmed on June 19. If she’s in Paris while the Swift-Kelce wedding is supposedly happening in Rhode Island...Was Madison Square Garden setup just a ploy? Or was it for the main guest lists, leaving Rhode Island just for their nearest and dearest? Only time will tell!
Article Summary: Kylie Kelce was spotted across pond from Swift-Kelce rumored wedding in Rhode Island. The 34-year-old mom-of-four even called out TMZ for swarming her mother- in-law at the LAX airport.

Article Title: "I’ll never get that time back": Taylor Swift’s subtle dig at her relationship with Joe Alwyn sparks chaos ahead of her wedding to Travis Kelce
Article Text:
Taylor Swift’s subtle dig at her relationship with Joe Alwyn sparks chaos ahead of her wedding to Travis Kelce.(Image via X)

Taylor Swift’s comments on her relationship with Joe Alwyn raises eyebrows ahead of her grand wedding to Travis Kelce



Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have always been pretty public about their high profile relationship and have never shied away from expressing their love for one another.But that was not the case when the billionaire pop star Taylor Swift was in a long term relationship with her ex boyfriend, popular Hollywood actor Joe Alwyn. Ahead of her wedding, her comments on being with Joe Alwyn have sparked chaos.Back in 2023, in an interview with TIME, when asked about her highly public relationship with Travis Kelce, Taylor Swift said, “When you say a relationship is public, that means I’m going to see him do what he loves, we’re showing up for each other, other people are there and we don’t care.”The billionaire pop star added, “The opposite of that is you have to go to an extreme amount of effort to make sure no one knows that you’re seeing someone. And we’re just proud of each other.”The “Life of a Showgirl” artist continued, “Me locking myself away in my house for a lot of years—I’ll never get that time back. I’m more trusting now than I was six years ago.”Interestingly, Taylor Swift’s comments have now resurfaced and many feel she was taking a subtle dig at the six long years she spent with Joe Alwyn in private.For the unversed, both Taylor Swift and Joe Alwyn were strictly private about their lives.The two met at MET Gala in 2016 and quickly got into a relationship.Since 2016, both Taylor Swift and Joe Alwyn avoided making public appearances together. Shockingly, the two were often spotted even running away from paparazzis at events as they remained private about their high profile relationship.However, when Taylor Swift ended things with Joe Alwyn in 2023, she became pretty public about her life. Her brief relationship with Matty Healy was relatively public before she fell in love with Travis Kelce.Three years later, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are all set to tie the knot next month after they have publicly supported and cheered on one another.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have always been pretty public about their high profile relationship. But that was not the case when the billionaire pop star Taylor Swift was in a long term relationship with her ex boyfriend, popular Hollywood actor Joe Alwyn. The two met at MET Gala in 2016 and quickly got into a relationship. Taylor Swift’s comments have now resurfaced and many feel she was taking a subtle dig at the six long years she spent with Alwyn in private.

Amid all the mess, the Detroit Lions have emerged as a potential landing spot for him.In the last few months, a number of rival teams have been linked to the Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson. As per NFL insiders, the Detroit Lions have been rumored to be interested in him.A few days ago, Fox Sports’ Greg Auman noted in a recent report, “Richardson actually has an 8-7 record as a starter, but a 50% career completion rate is alarming, with more career interceptions (13) than touchdown passes (11). He's rushed for 634 yards in 17 career games, but that's only a plus if you can improve his passing skills enough to keep him on the field.”Fox Sports’ Greg Auman added, “He's in a similar boat to Tennessee Titans quarterback Will Levis, pushed out by the team that drafted him and in the final year of his rookie contract, available for pennies on the dollar compared to his original draft value. POTENTIAL LANDING SPOT: Detroit Lions, who could use a developmental backup with Teddy Bridgewater as QB2.”For the Indianapolis Colts, trading Anthony Richardson will instantly clear up $5.385 million in cap space but the team will be hit with $5.43 million in dead cap.Interestingly, a new team trading for him would have to take on his guaranteed $5.385 million salary if the trade happens before 1st August.After 1st August, the Indianapolis Colts must pay his $4.24 million roster bonus themselves.For the Detroit Lions, trading for Anthony Richardson is pretty affordable for the team with over $18.5 million in cap space. The Indianapolis Colts’ star player would also be an impactful addition to the team as he would become a highly talented, low risk backup player to sit and learn behind starter Jared Goff.
